page: Amazon Web Services i3en.large is a storage-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 vCPUs, which correspond to 1 dedicated physical core supporting 2 threads. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. A key hardware component is the bundled local storage, consisting of a 1250 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between storage capacity and processing performance. The CPU performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core scoring 1318.68 (bottom 25%) and multi-core scoring 1731.97 (bottom 10%). Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block operations measured at 13.72 GB/sec and write operations at 11.8 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10%. Database and application benchmarks reflect these constraints; Passmark database operations scored 690.78, and Redis SET operations achieved 1.65 million operations per second, both placing in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i3en.large offers high local storage density and a generous memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring significant local NVMe capacity without demanding high compute power. Given the low CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks, this instance is best suited for localized database storage, basic caching, or data logging tasks where storage volume and low-latency NVMe access are prioritized over raw computational throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs on a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 1250 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a high storage-to-compute ratio but exhibits low overall processing performance. Benchmarks place its single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, with memory bandwidth and database operations also scoring in the bottom 10%. Consequently, the instance represents a qualitative tradeoff, offering dense local NVMe storage at the expense of computational speed. It is best suited for storage-heavy workloads, local database hosting, and caching applications that do not require intensive CPU processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 1250 GB NVMe SSD, offering high storage density but low CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.large is a storage-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 1250 GB NVMe SSD, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A storage-dense instance pairing 1250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for localized data workloads.
bullet_points: Features a 1250 GB local NVMe SSD for high-capacity local storage.
Equipped with 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz.
Includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for storage-heavy workloads and local databases with low CPU demands.
